observation_state_at_amendment.note: The measurement run was halted during repository cloning. No instance was scored, and no overlap or control count existed when this amendment was recorded.

amendments.title: Exclude the task's own fixing-PR tests from qualification
amendments.defect: SWE-bench `base_commit` is the parent of the pull request that fixes the task. Any forward window starting at `base_commit` therefore necessarily contains that pull request, whose `test_patch` adds tests referencing the touched symbols by construction. The sealed v1 qualification rule would have counted those tests, making the primary yield a tautology near 1.0 and the cross-repository control near 0.0.
amendments.why_it_matters: The measured effect would have been an artifact of dataset construction, not evidence of maintainer consequence-test behavior. It would also have been indistinguishable from a real result by inspection of the headline numbers alone.
amendments.correction: A test function is excluded from qualification when its file path and function name match a test function added by the instance's own `test_patch`. Those tests are the visible grader, not a hidden consequence test.

amendments.title: Add a within-repository backward-window control
amendments.defect: The sealed v1 control pairs an instance's symbols against a different repository's later tests. That control conflates two effects: whether the symbols are repository-specific vocabulary, and whether later tests actually target the task's code. Django symbols rarely appear in sympy tests for trivial reasons, so the v1 control is expected to be near zero regardless of whether the hypothesis is true.
amendments.why_it_matters: A control that cannot fail for the right reason inflates the apparent effect. The v1 control alone would have overstated the result.
amendments.correction: Add a second control on the same instance, same repository, and same window length, looking backward from `base_commit` instead of forward. It measures the rate at which the task's symbols appear in tests added before the task existed. Tests written before the fix cannot be consequence tests for it, so the forward-minus-backward difference isolates the temporal effect from repository vocabulary. Both controls are reported; neither replaces the other.

amendments.title: Require the primary to beat both controls
amendments.defect: The sealed v1 bar 2 required the primary to exceed only the cross-repository control.
amendments.correction: The screen passes only if the primary exceeds BOTH the cross-repository control and the backward within-repository control, each at one-sided exact conditional McNemar `p < 0.01` with a yield difference of at least `0.10`. Sealed bars 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7 are unchanged.
amendments.direction: strictly stricter than the sealed bar

known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.title: The backward control is exposed to repository growth
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.detail: Repositories accumulate testing activity as they mature, so a forward window can contain more added tests than a backward window of equal length for reasons unrelated to the task. The forward-minus-backward difference is therefore an upper bound on the temporal effect, not a clean estimate of it.
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.handling: Not corrected. Every result block reports an `exposure` diagnostic giving forward and backward added-test totals, medians, zero counts, and their ratio, so a reader can judge how much of any difference is exposure imbalance. A design that matches exposure is future work, not a post-hoc adjustment here.

known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.title: A1 leaks when a fixing-PR test is later relocated
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.detail: The task's own tests are excluded by matching file path and function name. If a later commit inside the window moves such a test to a different file, the moved copy no longer matches the exclusion key and is counted as a hidden consequence test.
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.handling: Not corrected. Excluding by bare function name instead would over-exclude unrelated tests sharing common names such as `test_basic`. The leak is reported as a known upward bias rather than traded for a larger one.
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.direction_of_bias: inflates the primary yield, expected small

known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.title: The screen has low sensitivity by construction, so a null is asymmetric
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.detail: Qualification requires a later test to contain a token exactly equal to a symbol the gold patch touched. Real consequence tests routinely exercise changed code through a public API and never name the internal symbol that changed. Verified concretely before any aggregate existed: instance `psf__requests-1142` touches `prepare_content_length`, an internal method a genuine regression test would exercise via `requests.post(...)` without ever writing that token. The instrument therefore detects only the subset of consequence tests that name the changed symbol.
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.handling: Not corrected; correcting it requires call-graph reachability or executed coverage, which is a separate container-bound gate this one does not authorize. Recorded before observation so the asymmetry cannot be argued after the fact.
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.direction_of_bias: deflates the primary yield; a null therefore understates the true rate

inference_rule_fixed_before_observation.if_pass: Later tests reference task-touched symbols above both controls. This is evidence FOR harvestable targeting and justifies building the executed-coverage gate.
inference_rule_fixed_before_observation.if_null: This falsifies STATIC NAME MATCHING as a detector. It does NOT falsify the harvest hypothesis, because the instrument cannot see public-API consequence tests. A null must not be reported as evidence that maintainer consequence tests are absent, and must not by itself send the mission to purchase human annotators.

known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.title: Bar 3 is partly a property of dataset composition
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.detail: The sealed bar requires that no single repository contribute more than 50 percent of qualifying instances. Django is 231 of 500 instances (46 percent), so under a uniform yield Django would approach that bar regardless of the phenomenon.
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.handling: Not corrected and not retuned. The bar is evaluated exactly as sealed and this weakness is reported with the result. Retuning a sealed bar after seeing the sample is the reward hack this repository exists to study.
known_limitations_disclosed_not_fixed.direction_of_bias: makes bar 3 uninformative rather than biased

unchanged: sample rule: every SWE-bench Verified instance, no subsampling, no discretionary exclusion
